title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Good morning, everybody! Today, I'm going to show you my daily routine, but Squid Game is themed. In this vlog, you'll learn new vocabulary words and natural English expressions that you can use in your everyday life. I recommend shadowing me as you watch to help improve your speaking and your pronunciation. I love starting my day off with a workout and I found a really interesting workout video on YouTube called Squid Gains. Gains is kind of like gym slang. It basically means muscle. So you can say something like, gotta get them gains. Or, ooh, she got gains. It means they're really fit and muscular.

Let's do this. This is the hard part. My legs are shaking, but I'm trying to keep them nice and straight and low to the ground. That workout really hurt my ego. I got to chug some water after that. I'm so dehydrated. I worked up an appetite, so now it's time to cook my favorite meal of the day breakfast. I'm going to make the exact same meal that they actually eat in squid games. I'm turning on the gas. I'm pouring some oil into the pan and I'll let that heat up for a little bit. I usually use avocado oil. I'm cracking my egg and being careful not to get any pieces of the egg shell in it, which I failed at. I have a small piece. Now I have to fish it out.
I'm going to use a rubber spatula to flip the egg.

This would be a perfect over easy egg, because I didn't break the yolk. While the egg is cooking, I'm going to prep for step two of this recipe. Ooh, that does not crack well on there. The egg is stubborn.
I'm going to try a bite of this first. I'm a little bit intimidated by these ones. I think the eyeballs just kind of creep me out, but here we go. Very salty. It doesn't taste fishy at all, actually.

Humvelled. I think I have to modify the last ones. I'll keep my knees on the ground.

Step two, I will be cooking this sausage. I have never tried this kind of sausage before in my life, so I'm actually very curious about it. I've got the sausage on my cutting board, and I'm cutting it into slices. I'm whisking the egg. First, you have to coat the sausage in flour, and then you dip it in the egg and put it in the pan.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: Step two, I will be cooking this sausage. I have never tried this kind of sausage before in my life, so I'm actually very curious about it. I've got the sausage on my cutting board, and I'm cutting it into slices. I'm whisking the egg. First, you have to coat the sausage in flour, and then you dip it in the egg and put it in the pan.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: I love how in Korea, sometimes when you get delivery, they'll leave little note on the packages.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Good morning, everybody! Today, I'm going to show you my daily routine, but Squid Game is themed. In this vlog, you'll learn new vocabulary words and natural English expressions that you can use in your everyday life. I recommend shadowing me as you watch to help improve your speaking and your pronunciation. I love starting my day off with a workout and I found a really interesting workout video on YouTube called Squid Gains. Gains is kind of like gym slang. It basically means muscle. So you can say something like, gotta get them gains. Or, ooh, she got gains. It means they're really fit and muscular.
Now I just have to stir fry some kimchi. Look, you can tell how much I love kimchi. I go through kimchi like crazy. I'm working so hard making this entire meal completely from scratch.

I'm excited. I'm going to try to get everything in one bite. It's tasty. Let me try one of these sausage slices. It tastes like fish sticks somehow. I love fish sticks, so I think it's delicious, but it's totally unexpected. I thought it would taste more like bacon or a hot dog. Now, I know I could survive in squid games. If they're feeding me like this, I think I'd be very happy.
I could never survive in squid games. Korean kids games are so difficult in the US.
Why is he beat me at everything? Korean! Have some mercy. I can go into a squid game and win some money.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: I might have to go to the dentist after eating this. It tastes really good, though.
I might like these even more. I'm going to give it 9.3. Taste kind of garlicky?

title: have to / must
examples: This workout video is an at-home, full-body, strength training video. And it's based on the game Red Light, Green Light, that's in Squid Gains. When the instructor says, Green Light, I have to continuously do a move. And when he says, Red Light, I have to stop and hold myself in that position. Step one is to turn on the AC because I'm already sweating in this track suit.
Humvelled. I think I have to modify the last ones. I'll keep my knees on the ground.

Now I just have to stir fry some kimchi. Look, you can tell how much I love kimchi. I go through kimchi like crazy. I'm working so hard making this entire meal completely from scratch.

title: had better / it's time
examples: Let's do this. This is the hard part. My legs are shaking, but I'm trying to keep them nice and straight and low to the ground. That workout really hurt my ego. I got to chug some water after that. I'm so dehydrated. I worked up an appetite, so now it's time to cook my favorite meal of the day breakfast. I'm going to make the exact same meal that they actually eat in squid games. I'm turning on the gas. I'm pouring some oil into the pan and I'll let that heat up for a little bit. I usually use avocado oil. I'm cracking my egg and being careful not to get any pieces of the egg shell in it, which I failed at. I have a small piece. Now I have to fish it out.

Now, it's time to play some games. Of course, we have to start with dockchi. This is the most iconic game in squid games. In the US, we don't have the same thing, but we have something kind of similar, called Pogs. It was popular when I was very young, but I never actually played Pogs.
I just checked Netflix and the new episodes are finally up. So it's time to watch season three of SquidGames. As you can see, I have a big variety of snacks here and they're all SquidGames themed.

It's time for a face mask. I'm ready. Boo. Can you tell who I am? It's the red light green light girl. I found this on Amazon and I couldn't resist. I got to stop laughing. The upper lip is going into my mouth. Are these her eyebrows? I guess this is like the eyeliner. They gave her something 90s brows. Red light green light. Are you scared? Why are you backing away? Where are you going?

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: I thought this would be an easy workout. Jokes on me. Next up is push-ups. Lower myself to the ground and push up. I'm more confident in my upper body strength.

This would be a perfect over easy egg, because I didn't break the yolk. While the egg is cooking, I'm going to prep for step two of this recipe. Ooh, that does not crack well on there. The egg is stubborn.

I'm excited. I'm going to try to get everything in one bite. It's tasty. Let me try one of these sausage slices. It tastes like fish sticks somehow. I love fish sticks, so I think it's delicious, but it's totally unexpected. I thought it would taste more like bacon or a hot dog. Now, I know I could survive in squid games. If they're feeding me like this, I think I'd be very happy.

Gotta roll up my sleeves for this. I thought games were supposed to be fun. I did use to play with tops when I was younger, but never with the rope. We would just do it where you grab this part and spin it, so I have no clue what I'm doing right now.
Really good. Ooh. I would vote X for sure. Oh, I do not like this lady.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: It's so cute. Look at that. Wow! It's a cream-filled waffle, but then it has little crunchy pieces of Dalgona on the top. It's really good, but I don't know if I could tell that it's Dalgona. It just tastes like a sweet latte to me. Delicious, though.

title: I wish I knew
examples: It tastes good but I don't like the texture. I wish it was softer or harder but it's an awkward in between. These get a seven. Oh my gosh is she actually giving... She actually had a baby during this game. What? She's a fighter, though. Broken ankle, having a baby.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: Gotta roll up my sleeves for this. I thought games were supposed to be fun. I did use to play with tops when I was younger, but never with the rope. We would just do it where you grab this part and spin it, so I have no clue what I'm doing right now.

title: have something done
examples: I got a Dalgona kit. This mini pan is so cute. I'm putting a spoonful of brown sugar.
I think I have the best shot at getting this one out though. It's a mini one.
